nodes.description: Skull-base invagination can occur in type IV OI and warrants radiographic surveillance when craniovertebral junction disease is suspected.

evidence.reference_title: Skull base abnormalities in osteogenesis imperfecta: a cephalometric evaluation of 54 patients and 108 control volunteers.
evidence.snippet: As a sign of basilar invagination the odontoid process protruded into the foramen magnum or reached the foramen magnum level in 22.2% of the patients with OI, whereas none of the controls showed this feature.

nodes.description: Bone pain is reported in adults with OI type IV and is a significant contributor to reduced quality of life in this population.

evidence.reference_title: Osteogenesis imperfecta
evidence.snippet: HP:0002653 | Bone pain | Frequent (79-30%)

evidence.reference_title: Incorporating the patient perspective in the study of rare bone disease.
evidence.snippet: Other areas included pain, gastrointestinal problems,

nodes.description: Long-bone bowing can occur in type IV OI, particularly in the femur.

evidence.reference_title: Management of Combined Fracture Neck of Femur and Femoral Deformity in Osteogenesis Imperfecta Patient: A Case Report.
evidence.snippet: The bone fragility and repeated fractures produce deformities of the long bones especially in femur and tibia. However, neck of femur (NOF) fractures in OI are rarely described. A 11-year-old male patient known to have OI (Sillence type IV) sustained a NOF fracture after a fall. He also had proximal femoral anterolateral bowing proximally and over an intramedullary (IM) rod inserted 4 years back.

nodes.description: Abnormal collagen that escapes intracellular quality-control is poorly secreted and disrupts extracellular matrix assembly and mineralization, weakening bone matrix integrity.

evidence.reference_title: Mutation analysis of COL1A1 and COL1A2 in patients diagnosed with osteogenesis imperfecta type I-IV.
evidence.snippet: Most patients with a clinical diagnosis of OI type I-IV have a mutation in the COL1A1 or COL1A2 genes which encode the two alpha chains of type I collagen, the major component of the bone matrix.

nodes.description: Gross motor development may become delayed after unsupported sitting is achieved.

evidence.reference_title: Osteogenesis imperfecta: profiles of motor development as assessed by a postal questionnaire.
evidence.snippet: In OI type IV, a retardation in motor development developed after the milestone 'sitting without support' was achieved.

nodes.description: Variable dentinogenesis imperfecta, sometimes with yellow-brown or opalescent gray tooth discoloration.

evidence.reference_title: Osteogenesis imperfecta with dominant inheritance and normal sclerae.
evidence.snippet: In Type 4 the first fracture more commonly occurs at birth, dentinogenesis imperfecta is more frequent than in Type 1 and bruising and nose-bleeds are less common.

evidence.reference_title: Dentinogenesis imperfecta in children with osteogenesis imperfecta: a clinical and ultrastructural study.
evidence.snippet: A total of ten patients had abnormal discolourations referable to DI: four patients were affected by OI type I, three patients by OI type III, and three patients by OI type IV. The discolourations, yellow/brown or opalescent grey, could not be related to the different types of OI.

nodes.description: Secondary signaling and resorption abnormalities further amplify fragility, with excessive TGF-beta signaling and increased osteoclast-related bone turnover superimposed on the abnormal collagen matrix.

evidence.reference_title: Excessive transforming growth factor-β signaling is a common mechanism in osteogenesis imperfecta.
evidence.snippet: Here, we show that excessive transforming growth factor-β (TGF-β) signaling is a mechanism of OI in both recessive (Crtap(-/-)) and dominant (Col1a2(tm1.1Mcbr)) OI mouse models. In the skeleton, we find higher expression of TGF-β target genes, higher ratio of phosphorylated Smad2 to total Smad2 protein and higher in vivo Smad2 reporter activity.

evidence.reference_title: Osteoclast indices in osteogenesis imperfecta: systematic review and meta-analysis.
evidence.snippet: Bone histomorphometry demonstrated the trends for higher osteoclast numbers (1.16; CI: -0.22, 2.55) and osteoclast surface (0.43; CI: -0.63, 1.49), and significantly higher eroded surface (3.24; CI: 0.51, 5.96) compared with age-matched controls.

nodes.description: Femoral bowing can occur in type IV OI from repeated fractures and impaired bone remodeling, though it is typically less severe than in type III.

evidence.reference_title: Osteogenesis imperfecta
evidence.snippet: HP:0002980 | Femoral bowing | Frequent (79-30%)

evidence.reference_title: Scoliosis in osteogenesis imperfecta caused by COL1A1/COL1A2 mutations - genotype-phenotype correlations and effect of bisphosphonate treatment.
evidence.snippet: III (89%), followed by OI type IV (61%) and OI type I (36%).

nodes.description: Conductive hearing impairment can occur in childhood in some patients.

evidence.reference_title: Hearing loss in children with osteogenesis imperfecta.
evidence.snippet: Two sporadic OI cases with conductive hearing loss were ascertained (4.4%): An 11-year-old girl with type IV OI with a PTA0.5-2 kHz of 35/40 dB HL and a 15-year-old boy with type IV OI with a PTA0.5-2 kHz of 27/18 dB HL.

nodes.description: Short stature is common, with reduced growth velocity during childhood and variable severity across affected individuals.

evidence.reference_title: Growth characteristics in individuals with osteogenesis imperfecta in North America: results from a multicenter study.
evidence.snippet: In children, the median z-scores for height in OI types I, III, and IV were -0.66, -6.91, and -2.79, respectively. Growth velocity was diminished in OI types III and IV.

nodes.description: Structural collagen I mutations disrupt procollagen folding, chaperone-assisted processing, and transport from the endoplasmic reticulum to the Golgi before abnormal collagen reaches the extracellular matrix.
nodes.id: Procollagen Processing and Intracellular Trafficking Defect

evidence.reference_title: Collagen transport and related pathways in Osteogenesis Imperfecta
evidence.snippet: The production of collagen entails a complex process, starting from the production of the collagen Iα1 and collagen Iα2 chains in the endoplasmic reticulum, during and after which procollagen is subjected to a plethora of posttranslational modifications by chaperones. After reaching the Golgi organelle, procollagen is destined to the extracellular matrix where it forms collagen fibrils.

nodes.description: Progressive hearing impairment can occur in type IV OI, though it is typically less common and later in onset than in type I.

evidence.reference_title: Osteogenesis imperfecta
evidence.snippet: HP:0001730 | Progressive hearing impairment | Frequent (79-30%)

evidence.reference_title: How common is hearing impairment in osteogenesis imperfecta?
evidence.snippet: hearing loss was significantly less common in the type IV disease than in the type I disorder.

nodes.description: Recurrent fractures are a core clinical feature, with fracture burden often declining during adolescence.

evidence.reference_title: Growth characteristics in individuals with osteogenesis imperfecta in North America: results from a multicenter study.
evidence.snippet: PURPOSE: Osteogenesis imperfecta (OI) predisposes people to recurrent fractures, bone deformities, and short stature.

evidence.reference_title: Osteogenesis imperfecta with dominant inheritance and normal sclerae.
evidence.snippet: The two types are similar in usually causing a mild disease but with a wide range of severity, and in both types the rate of fracture declines in adolescence.

nodes.description: Reduced BMD for age is well-documented in children and adults with type IV OI, contributing to fracture susceptibility and being the primary target of bisphosphonate treatment.

evidence.reference_title: Osteogenesis imperfecta
evidence.snippet: HP:0004349 | Reduced bone mineral density | Frequent (79-30%)

evidence.reference_title: Controlled trial of pamidronate in children with types III and IV osteogenesis imperfecta confirms vertebral gains but not short-term functional improvement.
evidence.snippet: treated patients experienced a significant increase in L1-L4 DXA z score (p < 0.001)

nodes.description: Scoliosis is a frequent spinal complication in type IV OI and may progress during growth.

evidence.reference_title: Scoliosis in osteogenesis imperfecta caused by COL1A1/COL1A2 mutations - genotype-phenotype correlations and effect of bisphosphonate treatment.
evidence.snippet: The prevalence of scoliosis was highest in OI type III (89%), followed by OI type IV (61%) and OI type I (36%).

nodes.description: Vertebral compression fractures are a clinically important complication in type IV OI, contributing to height loss and spinal deformity.

evidence.reference_title: Osteogenesis imperfecta
evidence.snippet: HP:0002953 | Vertebral compression fracture | Frequent (79-30%)

evidence.reference_title: Controlled trial of pamidronate in children with types III and IV osteogenesis imperfecta confirms vertebral gains but not short-term functional improvement.
evidence.snippet: pamidronate in children with types III and IV OI yielded positive vertebral

nodes.description: Wormian bones are a recognized cranial radiographic feature in the more deforming classical OI subtypes, including type IV.

evidence.reference_title: Skull base abnormalities in osteogenesis imperfecta: a cephalometric evaluation of 54 patients and 108 control volunteers.
evidence.snippet: These three abnormalities and wormian bones were predominantly found in OI Types III and IV as well as in patients exhibiting dentinal abnormality.
